skill.name: box-automation
skill.description: Automate Box cloud storage workflows through Rube MCP, including search, uploads, downloads, folders, sharing, collaborations, metadata queries, and Box Sign requests.
skill.summary: Run common Box file, folder, sharing, metadata, and signature workflows through Rube MCP.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported many Ruby backtick execution and weak cryptography patterns, but review found Markdown inline code, tool names, quoted parameter values, and examples rather than executable code. The only confirmed risk factor is network dependency on Rube MCP and Box web URLs; no prompt injection, credential exfiltration, or malicious intent was found.

low_findings.title: Markdown Inline Code Misclassified as Execution
low_findings.description: The static analyzer flagged many backtick-delimited Markdown fragments as Ruby shell execution. The reviewed lines contain tool names, parameter names, quoted values, and examples, not executable Ruby code.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The file is a Markdown skill guide and the flagged backticks wrap documentation tokens. I found no script blocks, Ruby source, or shell invocation instructions.

low_findings.title: Benign Network References
low_findings.description: The skill references the Rube MCP endpoint and example Box web URLs. These are expected for a Box automation guide, but users should understand it depends on external services.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The URLs are documented setup and ID-resolution examples. I found no evidence that the skill sends secrets or data to an unauthorized endpoint.

low_findings.title: Destructive Box Operations Are Documented
low_findings.description: The skill documents delete and permanent delete workflows for Box folders and files. This is expected functionality, but it can remove user content if used without confirmation.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The documented operations explicitly include delete and permanent remove tools. This is not malicious, but it is a real operational risk for users.

content.value_statement: Box teams often need repeated file, folder, search, and sharing tasks across large workspaces. This skill gives Claude, Codex, and Claude Code clear Rube MCP workflows for Box automation.
actual_capabilities: Guides Box OAuth connection setup through Rube MCP.
Maps upload, download, zip, and file information workflows to Box tools.
Explains search, folder browsing, metadata queries, and pagination parameters.
Documents folder creation, update, copy, trash, and permanent removal workflows.
Covers file sharing, collaboration roles, locks, and download permissions.
Lists Box Sign request lookup and cancellation workflows.
limitations: Requires an active Rube MCP server and a connected Box account.
Does not implement Box APIs directly or include executable scripts.
Standard upload guidance does not cover chunk uploads for files over 50 MB.
Available operations depend on Box permissions and enterprise settings.

use_cases.title: Organize shared project files
use_cases.description: Find project folders, upload current documents, and verify Box folder contents before team handoff.

use_cases.title: Audit collaboration access
use_cases.description: List file collaborations, review roles, and update access levels through documented Box workflows.

use_cases.title: Track signature requests
use_cases.description: List Box Sign requests, inspect request details, and cancel pending requests when needed.

prompt_templates.title: Find a Box file
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the Box automation skill to search for a file named quarterly budget in my Box workspace. Show the likely matches and file IDs.

prompt_templates.title: Upload to a folder
prompt_templates.prompt: Find the Box folder named Client Reports, verify its folder ID, then upload the local report file there using Rube MCP.

prompt_templates.title: Review sharing access
prompt_templates.prompt: Get the current collaborations for this Box file ID and summarize each collaborator, role, and invitation status.

prompt_templates.title: Plan a metadata query
prompt_templates.prompt: Build a Box metadata query for contracts in a specific ancestor folder. Include the required template format, query fields, and pagination approach.

output_examples.input: Search Box for onboarding documents and identify the folder that contains the newest version.
output_examples.output: The assistant searches Box, compares matching item metadata, and reports the best folder candidate with its folder ID and update time.

output_examples.input: Check who can access file 123456 and downgrade editors to viewers.
output_examples.output: The assistant lists current collaborations, identifies editor roles, asks for confirmation, and then updates selected collaborators to viewer access.

output_examples.input: Cancel a pending Box Sign request for the vendor agreement.
output_examples.output: The assistant lists matching sign requests, confirms the pending request ID, and cancels it only after user approval.

best_practices: Search Rube tools first so parameter schemas match the current Box connector.
Confirm file and folder IDs before uploads, moves, deletes, or sharing changes.
Use marker pagination for large Box folders and searches.
anti_patterns: Do not assume Box web URLs are direct API IDs without extraction.
Do not permanently delete folders or files without explicit user confirmation.
Do not skip pagination when a folder or search can return many items.

faq.question: Does this skill require a Box account?
faq.answer: Yes. It requires an active Box connection through Rube MCP before workflows can run.

faq.question: Does it include executable code?
faq.answer: No. The skill is a Markdown guide that maps Box tasks to Rube MCP tools.

faq.question: Can it upload large files?
faq.answer: It documents standard uploads. Files over 50 MB may require chunk upload APIs not covered by the standard tools.

faq.question: Can it manage Box permissions?
faq.answer: Yes. It documents collaboration listing, role updates, shared links, locks, and download permission settings.

faq.question: Can it search by metadata?
faq.answer: Yes. It explains metadata query fields, template naming, ancestor folders, and pagination considerations.

faq.question: Is it safe to publish?
faq.answer: Yes. Review found documentation-only content and no malicious instructions, with a low risk from external service dependency.
